title: Intense Sour Egyptian Rice Stew
description: A shaggy, sour stew that celebrates Egyptian flavors with parmesan, bonito shavings, and a dash of festive spice, simmered to perfection in a Dutch oven.
instructions: Heat the Dutch oven over medium heat and add the olive oil.
Sauté the diced onion until translucent, about 3 minutes.
Stir in the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add the tomato paste, lemon juice, and spice blend, mixing well and letting it simmer for 2 minutes.
Rinse the basmati rice until the water runs clear, then add it to the pot along with the water and chicken broth, and season with salt and pepper.
Bring to a boil, then reduce heat, cover, and simmer for 15 minutes until the rice is tender.
Stir in the grated parmigiano-reggiano and bonito shavings, allowing the cheese to melt into the stew.
Garnish with chopped parsley, serve hot, and enjoy the sour, festive flavor.
